subtasks.description: Evaluate emergency access request for user u_9981 (senior-engineer role) to production database db-payments-primary against access control and incident response policies. Verify on-call status, P1 incident justification, and determine authorization decision.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier because the decision requires multi-signal reasoning: role verification (senior-engineer qualifies), incident severity classification (P1 justifies emergency access), on-call status validation, and policy exception handling (emergency access policies typically have specific conditions). This is not a simple single-rule check (small), but involves multiple policy conditions and context that mid-tier capability handles well. No additional subtasks needed—this is a direct governance evaluation without requiring data fetching (those would be ad-hoc follow-ups if the policy decision is YES).

output.reason: User u_9981 (senior-engineer) has an active P1 incident ticket INC-20241215-0042, satisfying POL-501's incident ticket requirement. POL-502's dual-approval requirement is waived under the declared P1 incident exception. POL-503 requires mandatory post-access audit logging and review within 24 hours as a binding constraint on this grant.
applied_constraints: Access is strictly scoped to investigation activities related to incident INC-20241215-0042 only.
All read/write operations on db-payments-primary must be fully logged with timestamps and query details for the duration of this session.
Emergency access grant expires automatically after 4 hours or upon P1 incident closure, whichever comes first.
A post-access review must be completed within 24 hours of session termination per POL-503; reviewer must be DBA lead or security officer.
On-call status of u_9981 must be verifiable in the on-call roster at time of access; any discrepancy triggers immediate session revocation and escalation.
No bulk data exports, schema modifications, or destructive writes are permitted under this emergency grant.
Incident ticket INC-20241215-0042 must remain in active/open status for the duration of access; auto-revoke on ticket closure.
